title: `v-if` does not accept `string | undefined` — Type 'null' is not assignable to type 'Element'
author: dakefujo
body:
When using `v-if` with a `shallowRef`, the TypeScript compiler reports a type error. The directive seems to require a strictly non-nullable value as the conditional branch's return type, but Vue's internal rendering can return `null` when the condition is false. ```tsx import { defineComponent, shallowRef } from 'vue' export const Comp = defineComponent(() => { const str = shallowRef(undefined) return () => (

{str.value}

) }) ``` Error message. ``` Type 'VNode | Node | VaporFragment | DynamicFragment | VaporComponentInstance<{}, {}, StaticSlots, Record, Block, Record> | Element[] | null' is not assignable to type 'Element'. Type 'null' is not assignable to type 'Element'. ts(2322) ``` - vue 3.6.0-beta.10 - vue-jsx-vapor 3.2.10

title: `defineVaporComponent` not found in `vue/index.mjs` — v3.2.10 regression
author: TiBianMod
body:
Starting from `v3.2.10`, the following error is thrown at runtime in an SSR context using `@vue-jsx-vapor/compiler-rs`:

```
SyntaxError: Export named 'defineVaporComponent' not found in module '.../node_modules/vue/index.mjs'.
```

This did not occur on the previous version. Downgrading resolves the issue.

**Environment:**
- `vue-jsx-vapor`: `3.2.10`
- `@vue-jsx-vapor/compiler-rs`: latest
- `vue`: `3.6.0-beta.10`
- Runtime: `Bun`

**Working combination:**
- `vue-jsx-vapor`: `3.2.9`
- `vue`: `3.6.0-beta.10`

**Expected behavior:** No error — same as previous versions.

```

title: Children not rendered when `onClick` is set on button in JSX

title: File name filter is possibly too strict
author: JakeIsMeh
body:
I was trying this out alongside TanStack Router Vue and ran into transpilation issues when using TSR's autoCodeSplitting (TanStack/router#6760).

Using Vite@8, I would get a `"[UNTRANSPILED_SYNTAX] Error: JSX syntax should be transpiled before bundling. Found untranspiled JSX in "src/main.tsx"` error.

Upon further debugging, I began to suspect that this plugin, along with the way TSR requests for code-split files (by appending `?tsr-split=component` to file name) was the cause, since swapping for @vitejs/plugin-vue-jsx works fine.

However, trying to workaround this with a regex that allows for query params (`/\.[cm]?[jt]sx?(\?.*)?$/`) in plugin.options.includes, compiler-rs panics with `UnknownExtension("Please provide a valid file extension: .js, .mjs, .jsx or .cjs for JavaScript, or .ts, .d.ts, .mts, .cts or .tsx for TypeScript")`.

*repro can be found in linked issue

title: Support dynamic components
author: chbybnwr
body:
It's working with regular mode: [Playground](https://play.vuejs.org/#)

But, not with Vapor mode: [Playground](https://repl.zmjs.dev/vuejs/vue-jsx-vapor#)

Got these errors:
```
TypeError: Cannot create property '__proxyed' on string 'span'
```
```
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'anchor')
```

Reference: [Dynamic Components](https://vuejs.org/guide/essentials/component-basics.html#dynamic-components)

title: tsconfig 配置 vue-jsx-vapor 在webstorm下不生效
author: xiaozhu2024
body: 在tsconfig.json中配置 ```json "jsxImportSource": "vue-jsx-vapor", ``` 后 webstorm无法正常识别 在vscode正常的， 改回'vue'是正常的， 大致看了一下vue的jsx-runtime的实现，好像咱们的与vue的实现不太一样，导致的返回值类型的报错，大佬，有没有好的处理方案或者可以兼容一下吗 https://github.com/vuejs/core/blob/main/packages/vue/jsx-runtime/index.d.ts

title: Cannot destructure property 'ensureValidVNode' from null or undefined value
author: TiBianMod
body:
@zhiyuanzmj 
with `vue 3.6.0-beta.5` we have a new issue

```bash
Cannot destructure property 'ensureValidVNode' from null or undefined value
```

```bash
101 | 	return renderComponentVNode((0, vue.createVNode)(comp, props, children), parentComponent, slotScopeId);
102 | }
103 |
104 | //#endregion
105 | //#region packages/server-renderer/src/helpers/ssrRenderSlot.ts
106 | const { ensureValidVNode } = vue.ssrUtils;
                                   ^
TypeError: Cannot destructure property 'ensureValidVNode' from null or undefined value
      at <anonymous> (.../node_modules/@vue/server-renderer/dist/server-renderer.cjs.js:106:30)
      at <anonymous> (.../node_modules/@vue/server-renderer/index.js:6:10)
```

[Repo](https://github.com/TiBianMod/bun-vue-vapor)
Branche: [vue/ssrUtils](https://github.com/TiBianMod/bun-vue-vapor/tree/vue/ssrUtils)

> https://github.com/vuejs/vue-jsx-vapor/issues/39#issuecomment-3832758917

title: More redundant _normalizeVNode calls
author: KaelWD
body:
Following 6e4613961c0ca82541af792c289c25f8873dd43a, `{ foo && (
)}` still emits ```js (_temp = foo, _temp) ? (_openBlock(), _createBlock(_Fragment, { key: 0 }, [_normalizeVNode(() => (_openBlock(), _createElementBlock("div")))], 64)) : (_openBlock(), _createBlock(_Fragment, { key: 1 }, [_normalizeVNode(_temp)], 64)) ``` is `_normalizeVNode` necessary on `_createElementBlock`?

title: bug: some condition expressions are still static
author: KaelWD
body: a6c0cb53481089f53b421134cb8d6bff427d83db fixed ternaries but `??` `&&` and `||` still emit `_normalizeVNode(_temp)` not `_normalizeVNode(() => _temp)`

title: Teleport 功能和 @vitejs/plugin-vue-jsx 效果不一样
author: tujianglin

title: transform `TSX/JSX` files using `Bun.Plugin`
author: TiBianMod
body:
@zhiyuanzmj have a little patience with me and let's go step-by-step.

>  at the moment I have a project using `bun, inertiajs, vue-jsx, @babel/core, @vue/babel-plugin-jsx` and many other pkg.
The goal is to remove completly the usage of `@babel/core` and use `@vue-jsx-vapor/compiler-rs` to transform the `tsx` files,
but until now I encounter many issues to make this change.

[Reproduction repo](https://github.com/TiBianMod/bun-vue-vapor)
> using `@babel/core and @vue/babel-plugin-jsx` is working very well, but the goal is to use the new "Vapor mode"

**let's keep track of issues we will encounter**
- [ ] SyntaxError: Export named 'defineVaporComponent' not found in module '....'
- [x] SyntaxError: Export named 'renderEffect' not found in module './node_modules/vue/index.mjs'
- [x] SyntaxError: Export named 'ssrRegisterHelper' not found in module './node_modules/vue-jsx-vapor/dist/index.js'.
